title: Result - Sweden
content:
Welcome to Sweden, a land where nature and culture blend seamlessly. Thanks to Allemansrätten, the right of public access, everyone can explore the outdoors, camp and pick berries or mushrooms freely.
Sweden’s 29 national parks and nearly 4,000 nature reserves offer endless activities like hiking, skiing, fishing and mountain biking. Immerse yourself in Sweden’s vibrant cultural scene with concerts, plays and exhibitions year-round. While Swedish is the official language, most Swedes are fluent in English and many Swedish multinational companies operate in English.

## Unique food tradition
Surströmming (pickled herring)
Sweden has a strong economy and many Swedish multinational organizations speak English as their corporate language.

### Local legend or myth
The Tomte: a mythical creature, often compared to a gnome or elf, that is said to protect homes and farms in rural Sweden.

### Language of the Land
In 2009, Swedish was officially recognized as Sweden's national language, despite being spoken there for centuries. However, the vast majority of Swedes speak English.
islands make Sweden the country with the most islands in the world.
